context.product: Axion Designer, a local design-direction and proof system that helps teams shape, inspect, verify, and preserve production Web UI.
audience: Frontend engineers who can build interfaces but need concrete art direction
Product designers and design-system leads who need implementation evidence and drift control
Newcomers who need plain-language guidance instead of unexplained design jargon
context.task: Move from product intent to a distinctive responsive interface, inspect the real rendered states, and decide whether the implementation has enough evidence to ship.
context.action: Open the local Studio and review the current direction, proof matrix, and blocking findings
constraints: Keep subjective taste guidance separate from deterministic pass or fail evidence
Never call a route or state proven unless a fresh complete clean artifact and passing ledger attest it
Keep Studio local and read-only; preview code stays in an opaque sandbox
Support keyboard use, readable contrast, reduced motion, mobile layouts, and 200 percent zoom

identity.thesis: Make design intent, rendered UI, and verification evidence readable as one calm working surface.
identity.signature: A cobalt proof rail connects the chosen direction to concrete routes, states, tokens, and release evidence without turning the workspace into a monitoring dashboard.
identity.composition: Use one broad working canvas and one narrower evidence rail, joined by aligned rules and labels rather than a grid of interchangeable cards.
identity.responsive: On narrow screens, place the current verdict and next action first, then stack direction, preview, and evidence without changing their decision order.
identity.typography: Use the system sans for controls and guidance, a restrained book serif for direction statements, and monospace only for hashes, paths, tokens, and measured evidence.
identity.color: Use clean paper and quiet ink for the workspace, cobalt for the one primary action and proof rail, green only for verified evidence, and red only for blocking failures.
identity.form: Prefer crisp structural rules, modest radii, dense evidence rows, and clearly bounded transient panels; depth appears only where interaction requires layering.
identity.motion: Animate only view changes, disclosure, and inspector focus with short transitions; preserve meaning when reduced motion is requested.
identity.media: Use real UI screenshots, system graphs, and small functional diagrams; avoid decorative generated filler where product evidence should appear.
avoid: decorative gradients, glass, glow, and neon command-center styling
vanity quality scores that hide missing evidence
generic AI typography and placeholder copy
calling source declarations or unverified screenshots proof of execution
